scenario: Pinnacle Insurance, an insurer correlating 25000 events per hour to active process instances in Camunda 8.8, runs a custom inbound connector. A reviewer wants to verify how the executable should react when correlateWithResult returns a Failure, because the handling strategy is what decides whether the connector should report the problem upstream or swallow it silently.
question: How does the SDK describe the role of handlingStrategy on the CorrelationResult returned by correlateWithResult?

options.text: handlingStrategy is a Zeebe broker hint that controls how long the correlation is retried inside the engine before the subscription is closed and the executable is marked as failed by the runtime
handlingStrategy is a fixed legacy enum that returns the same constant for every failure, with the runtime always logging the error and never forwarding it to the upstream system in any case
handlingStrategy is derived by the connector runtime from user configuration and tells the executable whether a Failure should be forwarded to the upstream system or simply ignored

handlingStrategy is a callback the executable must invoke at activate time to register custom handlers, after which the runtime stops returning Failure values for the rest of the subscription lifecycle

a.text: Incorrect. handlingStrategy is not a broker retry hint; it is delivered with each CorrelationResult and only tells the executable how to react to a failed correlation.

b.text: Incorrect. The strategy can take more than one value and the runtime explicitly supports forwarding errors upstream, so it is neither a constant nor a deprecated field.

c.text: Correct. The docs state that the handling strategy is derived by the connector runtime based on user configuration and defines whether a Failure should forward the error to the upstream system or ignore it, which is exactly how the example handleResult branches on ForwardErrorToUpstream and Ignore.

d.text: Incorrect. handlingStrategy is a value on the failure result, not a callback registered in activate, and Failure values continue to be possible throughout the subscription lifecycle.

explanation: When correlateWithResult returns a Failure, the CorrelationResult carries a handlingStrategy that the runtime derives from user configuration. The executable inspects that strategy to decide whether to forward the error to the upstream system (ForwardErrorToUpstream) or to ignore it (Ignore).

docs.title: Connector SDK - Inbound connector runtime logic
